assets.alt: Flat liquid-glass weekly tray with an employee profile, post slips, and buyer signal on a mint mesh background
assets.notes: Generated with Codex image generation and saved to the owned post asset directory.
assets.final_prompt: Use case: stylized-concept. Asset type: wide blog hero. Source context from article: The article explains how to scale employee-generated content on LinkedIn by choosing a small credible employee group, capturing expertise weekly, keeping review light, and measuring buyer response. The main idea is repeatable support for employee voice, not a busy content dashboard. Primary request: Create one simple visual metaphor inferred from the article source context: a single flat liquid-glass weekly tray holding one employee profile symbol and three quiet post slips, with one small buyer-signal mark nearby. Use one or two relevant elements only. Do not show a workflow map, operating dashboard, content calendar grid, or many employees. Style reference: Use the Sell In Public Flat Liquid Mesh Hero style: generated raster PNG, softly blended mesh gradient background using soft mint as the main color plus a close pale teal complement, matte translucent liquid-glass shapes, simple head-on flat UI, consistent white outline weight, clean negative space, and subtle grain. Composition: Wide landscape hero near 1600x700. Place one central flat glass subject with quiet space around it. Keep the graphic readable as a single concept, not an infographic or screenshot. Scene/backdrop: Soft mint and pale teal mesh gradient only. Blend colors smoothly without hard edges. Avoid multi-color mesh palettes. Subject: One simple weekly tray with an employee profile symbol, three abstract post slips, and one small buyer-signal dot. Markings must be abstract and non-readable: short white lines and simple shapes only. Present the graphic completely flat and head-on, with no angled or isometric view. Detail density: Low. Simple, spacious, and specific. Avoid decorative filler. Constraints: Generated raster PNG style only. No readable text, logos, watermarks, exact LinkedIn UI, fake metrics, scattered nodes, random lines, icon clouds, fake dashboards full of metrics, overcomplicated UIs, generic LinkedIn-post-card default, glossy 3D objects, glow, bloom, flares, light trails, shiny/specular/reflection cues, bokeh/orbs, hard gradient edges, SVG/vector look, photoreal stock-photo look, more than two background colors, angled perspective, isometric view, tilted panels, or three-quarter UI view.
